name: GaugeFlow Gate A5 quotient substrate repair v1
objective: Repair only the mathematically mismatched atom-type and coordinate probability paths identified by Gate A4, then re-qualify the generator substrate with endpoint IDs before any tensor-conditioned experiment.

historical_evidence_is_immutable.instruction: A/A1/A2/A3/A4 reports, thresholds, splits, checkpoints and failure conclusions are immutable. A5 is a new substrate protocol, not a rerun or reinterpretation.

gate_a4: Analytic closure passed for all paths, excluding a sampler direction/representation identity defect.
Endpoint-ID type composition was 0.000 and geometry retrieval was 0.5625, so tensor conditioning is not the causal bottleneck.
A4's softmax-Gaussian/simplex proxy lacked exact simplex retraction, tangent projection and an endpoint decoder objective.
A4's coordinate target was tied to CIF atom order and absolute fractional translation, although the physical crystal state is permutation and global-translation invariant.

literature.citation: Miller et al., FlowMM: Generating Materials with Riemannian Flow Matching, 2024
literature.use: Adopt a true Dirichlet simplex base, exact simplex retraction/tangent projection, no-drift torus quotient, and endpoint type decoder objective; do not restore FlowMM as a runtime dependency.

literature.citation: Gat et al., Discrete Flow Matching, 2024
literature.use: Supports a posterior/endpoint-prediction view of discrete generation; A5 implements the simpler FlowMM-style simplex endpoint objective, not a continuous-time Markov sampler.

literature.citation: Lipman et al., Flow Matching for Generative Modeling, 2023; Pooladian et al., Multisample Flow Matching, 2023
literature.use: Supports replacing arbitrary independent source-target pairing by an explicit lower-cost coupling. A5 uses training-only periodic Hungarian coupling, never a target structure at sampling.

endpoint_id_substrate.condition: two-class one-hot endpoint ID only; no tensor, orbit, oracle, target CIF, target lattice or target symmetry condition reaches the model

decision_rules.type_variant_failure: Do not return to tensor conditioning. Evaluate a separately proposed discrete-flow/CTMC replacement, not target-specific vocabulary pruning.
decision_rules.geometry_variant_failure: Do not return to tensor conditioning. Diagnose lattice quotient/coordinate architecture after confirming OT and translation quotient invariants.
decision_rules.joint_only_failure: Do not return to tensor conditioning. Audit joint-head interference with frozen scale diagnostics.
decision_rules.all_criteria_pass: Only then may a separately versioned tensor-conditioned gate be proposed; A5 never itself passes Gate A.
